opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was watching a surgery scene on TV, my stomach flipped, my vision tunneled, and the next thing I knew I was on the floor.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I suddenly fainted while watching a medical show on TV and had a small fall, and my partner got really frightened by how quickly it happened.

symptom_narrative.onset: it came on suddenly while I was standing in the warm living room watching a surgery scene on TV
symptom_narrative.duration: I was only out for a very short time and came round within about 20 seconds once I was lying flat on the floor
symptom_narrative.character: my stomach flipped and I felt really sick and sweaty and hot all over, then my vision tunnelled and my ears were ringing, and then I was on the floor
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: frightening because I actually passed out and fell, but I feel okay now, just really drained and washed-out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the whole thing came over my whole body; there's no particular pain anywhere
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it was a single episode that built up over several seconds with clear warning signs first
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying flat on the floor - I came round within about 20 seconds of being down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: watching the blood/surgery on TV set it off, and the room was quite warm; I'd been standing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: before it I felt very nauseous, was pouring sweat, felt hot all over, my vision narrowed to a tunnel and my ears were roaring/ringing; my partner said I went really pale and just went limp; no chest pain and I didn't notice my heart racing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't think this exact thing has happened before, though I've always been a bit squeamish about blood
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm fully awake and talking normally, resting on the sofa, just feeling drained and washed-out

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: I don't take any regular medications

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: the living room was quite warm and I'd been up and standing while the TV was on; I've always been a bit squeamish at the sight of blood

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants reassurance that fainting at the sight of blood is harmless and not a sign of a heart or brain problem, and to settle their frightened partner

communication_style.typical_message_style: gives a clear account of the warning signs and can relay what the spouse witnessed

objective_data_offered.value: my spouse says I suddenly went very pale and limp and slid down onto the carpet, then came round within about 20 seconds once I was lying flat
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander recollection during a frightening event; timing is approximate

objective_data_offered.value: I don't think I hurt myself in the fall - I landed on the carpet and can't feel any sore spots or bumps; no cuts
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-inspection only; not a clinician examination for head or other injury
